# AI Translation Trends That Will Dominate in 2023 and Beyond

![AI Translation services](https://future-trans.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/05/creative-artificial-intelligence-concept-with-human-brain-sketch-modern-digital-tablet-desktop-background-top-view-double-exposure-min-1024x683.jpg)

#### Table of Contents

translation grows ever more pressing. In recent years, advances in artificial intelligence (AI) have dramatically improved the quality of machine translation, making it more accessible and useful than ever before. In this blog, we’ll explore some of the AI translation trends that are likely to dominate in 2023 and beyond.  
  
According to a report by Research and Markets, the global machine translation market was valued at $520.9 million in 2020 and is expected to reach $1.5 billion by 2026, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 18.3% during the forecast period. Another report by Technavio forecasts the global language translation software market to grow by $4.51 billion during 2021-2025, with a CAGR of over 16%. These statistics demonstrate the significant growth potential of AI translation, as businesses and organizations increasingly rely on these technologies to communicate with global audiences, improve their workflows, and reduce translation costs.

## 5 AI Translation Trends That Will Dominate in 2023 and Beyond

Neural machine translation, or NMT, is a type of machine translation that uses neural networks to predict the most likely translation of a sentence or phrase. NMT has rapidly become the dominant form of machine translation, thanks to its superior accuracy and ability to learn from vast amounts of data. In 2023, we can expect to see continued growth in the use of NMT, as more companies and organizations adopt this technology to improve their translation workflows.

Cloud-based translation services are becoming increasingly popular as businesses seek to streamline their translation workflows and reduce costs. These services allow users to access translation tools and resources from anywhere with an internet connection, making it easier to collaborate on translation projects and improve overall translation quality. In 2023, we can expect to see continued growth in the use of cloud-based translation services, as businesses and individuals alike seek to take advantage of these powerful tools.

Thanks to advances in AI and machine learning, machine translations are becoming more accurate and natural-sounding than ever before. In 2023, we can expect to see continued improvements in the quality of machine translations, as AI algorithms become more sophisticated and are trained on larger and more diverse datasets. As a result, machine translations will be increasingly able to capture the nuances of language and produce translations that are indistinguishable from those produced by human translators.

AI translation is just one of many AI technologies that are transforming the way we communicate and work. In 2023, we can expect to see greater integration between [AI translation](https://future-trans.com/machine-translation-post-editing-services/) and other AI technologies, such as speech recognition and natural language processing. This integration will make it possible to create more seamless and efficient translation workflows, and enable new use cases for machine translation in fields such as healthcare, finance, and law.

As [the global economy](https://www.imf.org/en/Publications/WEO) continues to expand, new languages and translation markets are emerging all the time. In 2023, we can expect to see continued growth in the demand for [translation services](https://future-trans.com/translation-services/) for languages such as Mandarin, Hindi, and Arabic, as well as for less commonly spoken languages in emerging markets. To meet this demand, we can expect to see new AI translation tools and services specifically tailored to these languages and markets.

### **Discover the Benefits of Future Trans AI Translation Services**

Future Trans is a [leading provider of AI translation services](https://future-trans.com/), offering a wide range of solutions to meet the needs of businesses and organizations around the world. With a team of experienced linguists and cutting-edge AI technology, Future Trans is able to deliver accurate, natural-sounding translations in over 100 languages. Their cloud-based translation platform makes it easy to collaborate on translation projects, track progress, and access translation resources from anywhere with an internet connection. Whether you need to translate documents, websites, or multimedia content, Future Trans has the expertise and technology to get the job done quickly and efficiently. With their commitment to quality, security, and customer satisfaction, Future Trans is the ideal partner for businesses seeking to expand their global reach through AI translation.

# The impact of translation technology in 2023

As we move into the new year, it’s natural to reflect on the past and look ahead to the future. 2022 has seen rapid growth in language technologies, with advancements in machine learning and AI.

In 2022, Google Lens developed [multi-search](https://blog.google/products/search/multisearch/), allowing users to perform searches based on images, objects and text. Apple made improvements to their [in-camera translations](https://www.macrumors.com/how-to/translate-text-camera-app-ios/), enabling you to point your phone camera at text and translate it in real-time. And [Meta’s Universal Speech Translator](https://tech.fb.com/artificial-intelligence/2022/10/ai-translation-unwritten-language/) was introduced, to convert unwritten language into spoken English.

As companies continue to develop these translation tools, the implications for communication in 2023 are far-reaching. Here we’ve listed our predictions for  2023’s innovations in language technology, and what they might mean for the professional language service industry.

## **3 Translation technologies to keep an eye on**

### **Augmented reality translations**

With augmented reality (AR) technology, you can point your phone’s camera at a sign or printed text, and have the translation appear in a virtual overlay. No typing or copy-pasting required. Now, you may be thinking this technology isn’t new, and you’d be right. Many apps were developed in the 2010s enabling users to take a photo of foreign text and read it in their own language.

However, AI object recognition and mobile processing speeds have come a long way in recent months. Apple’s latest advert (below) shows us a blind iPhone user navigating his environment using object recognition technology. 

### **Artificial intelligence-supported translations**

AI translation technology could help people who are learning a language, detecting errors in grammar and suggesting other formulations more suited to the context. An example of this already in place is [Google’s Neural Machine Translation (GNMT)](https://research.google/pubs/pub45610/), which can detect the language of a text and then translate it in real-time. This technology is being used for certain types of content by companies such as Microsoft, Amazon, Twitter and Apple.

The accuracy of these services will increase as more data is collected. We expect that, going forward, AI-assisted translations will become an integral part of our lives, in areas such as healthcare, education, and hospitality. 

### **Real-time conversational translations**

Voice translation is already widely available, with voice recognition services like Alexa and Google Home converting spoken words into written text. In 2023, we expect to see more companies introducing real-time voice translation into their software. In theory, going forward, this technology could enable casual users to converse at a basic level with others speaking a different language. 

## **The future of professional language services**

The language industry will continue to play a critical role in the development and adoption of new translation technology. Many language service providers are already investing heavily in AI-powered translation solutions and machine-learning technologies to improve accuracy and reduce costs. We expect this trend to continue so that businesses can turn around faster, more accurate services at competitive prices.

**Here’s our list of what to expect from language service providers in 2023:**

### **Improved customer service**

Through AI-powered translation and automated quality assurance, language service providers can enhance their customer experience. At Global Lingo, we incorporate a number of language technologies when and where they add value. 

### **Faster turnaround times**

AI-powered translation technologies allow linguists to quickly and accurately translate content, making it possible for businesses to deliver high-quality translations at pace. We expect this trend to continue as the technology continues to advance and more companies invest in machine learning solutions. This is perfect for businesses that need fast turnarounds without compromising on quality.

### **Data analysis and insights**

AI-powered translations offer a wealth of data for analysis and insight. Companies can now access real-time reports and analytics on their content, allowing them to make more informed decisions about their translation strategies. We are already seeing companies use this data to inform their marketing activities and optimise their international presence.

## **Human translators are still essential to businesses**

Though AI and machine learning have been instrumental in enhancing the turnaround and consistency of translations, they are no replacement for human linguists. Language professionals will continue to play a critical role in the industry, providing accurate, quality translations that capture the subtle nuances and cultural sensitivities of language. 

## **The future of language technology**

In conclusion, with advancements in new translation technology and a drive for businesses to become more customer-centric, there is a great deal of potential for growth and change in 2023. As the industry continues to grow, so too will the need for new tools and technologies.

At Global Lingo, we already incorporate the best translation technology available, enabling us to deliver projects at pace, while maintaining the accuracy and quality we’re proud to be recognised for. Our [machine translation](https://global-lingo.com/technology/machine-translation/) (MT), [translation memory & terminology management](https://global-lingo.com/technology/translation-memory-terminology-management/), and [automated speech recognition](https://global-lingo.com/technology/automated-speech-recognition/) (ASR) are working examples of our investments in the future of translation technologies. 

We are already beginning to see the real-world application of AI-assisted translation, real-time voice translation, data analysis and insights, all of which provide exciting opportunities to expand global reach. We look forward to the future of language technology and what it can bring to global business.

If you would like to see how we can help you make use of technology for your business communication, [contact one of our language specialists](https://global-lingo.com/enquiry/) today. We’ll be able to provide a comprehensive overview of how you can use our language services to enhance your businesses in 2023, and beyond.

# Top Machine Translation Software Trends for 2023

**Machine translation software** is the best solution for companies that are internationalizing their business and need to build a strong relationship with wider audiences, languages, and cultures. All this while guaranteeing data protection and complying with relevant regulations.

The software is currently being developed based on **artificial intelligence (AI)** to increase the speed, quality, and accuracy of translation and to offer technical and specialized linguistic services.

It can translate content in text, video, or image format; there are even tools that integrate SEO functionalities for web optimization in different languages. But it is always important to be familiar with what else to expect from this technology and the latest trends in [machine translation software](https://pangeanic.com/translation-technology/machine-translation-technology/).

Recommended reading:

[Everything you need to know about machine translation](/everything-about-machine-translation)

## How can machine translation technology help you?

Language barriers have been broken down for good, and **machine translation software** has played a fundamental role. Thanks to these engines, we are now able to communicate online at a global level, be it through websites, emails, social networks, forums, or mobile applications, etc.

This globalization has had a favorable impact on several aspects of business models. For example, it has facilitated companies' expansion and has made it possible to localize websites for their optimal adaptation to new cultures and target audiences.

It is possible to produce business documents in different languages using machine translation software. It also allows companies to publish new content faster, and streamlines web adaptation and the translation of software.

### Categories

Taking into account the evolution of machine translation technology, two main categories can be distinguished:

Statistical machine translation software (SMT): this type of software was developed in the 1990s using advanced statistical models. It was one of the first technologies used by Google to democratize information.

Neural machine translation software (NMT): this translation tool started being used in 2017. It employs the great capabilities of AI and [neural networks](https://blog.pangeanic.es/redes-neuronales-de-traduccion-automatica) to obtain more natural translations. Its content processing is similar to that of the human brain.

Recommended reading:

[How to train your machine translation engine](/_hcms/analytics/search/conversion?redirect=aHR0cHM6Ly9ibG9nLnBhbmdlYW5pYy5jb20vdHJhaW4tbWFjaGluZS10cmFuc2xhdGlvbi1lbmdpbmU%3D&ct=SEARCH&pid=14509501&cid=57959286826&t=aG93IHRvIHRyYWluIHlvdXIgbWFjaGluZSB0cmFuc2xhdGlvbiBlbmdpbmU%3D&d=blog.pangeanic.com&c=1&c=2&c=3&c=6&rp=1&ab=false&opcid=&rs=UNKNOWN&hs-expires=1718182630&hs-version=1&hs-signature=APUk-v6uhRI1O06etdtb2a3NNc4bRdH__w)

## 

## **The growing demand for machine translation software**

The demandformachine translation software has increased immensely during the past few years. Not only common languages such as English, Chinese, or Spanish are of interest; there is also a growing interest in other languages, such as Arabic. What is the reason for this?

**Developments in technology and globalization** have driven companies to target larger and larger international audiences. As a result, the use of machine translation tools that allow organizations to streamline their business activities and be more productive is becoming increasingly popular.

[Discover PageaMT, our machine translation software.](https://languagetechnology.pangeanic.com/)

## Machine translation software trends for 2023

It is indisputable that **neural machine translation software** is the tool best suited to the specific requirements of any translation. The trends observed are as follows:

### CAT tools and artificial intelligence

Computer-assisted translation (CAT) tools are actually not a recent development. However, their use has increased significantly and there is an array of new features available. Approximately 88% of translation professionals use at least one of these systems.

They are an easy-to-use support tool for the translation process. Text is segmented for translation and can be saved for reuse in future projects.

AI and neural networks are used to predict sentences or expressions and to use automatic translation plugins. These features can be integrated into different platforms.

Read more:

[Human-in-the-loop (HITL); making the most of human and machine intelligence](/human-in-the-loop-hitl-making-the-most-of-human-and-machine-intelligence)

### GDPR

Although the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)is not a translation tool, it is one of the most significant trends because it impacts the way a company processes sensitive and personal data. Consequently:

Any company whose customers are European citizens must translate their privacy policies into the necessary languages so that they can be understood.

Translation tools must be developed with certain levels of security to ensure compliance with privacy regulations.

More information:

[How to Comply With the GDPR When Processing Anonymized Data](/how-to-comply-with-gdpr-when-processing-anonymized-data)

### Machine translation technology

Machine translation platforms are a current trend because they make it easier for companies to expand through specialized translation services (such as software translation) and transcreation to adapt commercial, technical, or advertising content to each new culture and language.

The translation platform is a kind of interpreting machine that provides an automated process through the use of AI. It manages the localization process, creates a translation memory (TM), tracks updates, and allows the user to customize and monitor the way it runs.

### Fast and efficient management with translation memories

A [translation memory](https://pangeanic.com/translation-technology/translation-memory-technology/) (TM) is a database of compiled information from each previous process. Their use speeds up translations by matching fragments or sentences to the input content.

They are not to be confusedwith machine translation software, which uses algorithms with more advanced technology. But TMs, together with the MT, can complement each other for faster processing.

## Tips for choosing the right machine translation software

A few things to take into account when choosing [machine translation software](https://languagetechnology.pangeanic.com/) are:

The type of content and quality required 

The speed of processing

Price

Languages

Depending on the content, machine translation technology can be used in conjunction with human translation. This results in high quality, precision, and speed for each project.

## The future of machine translation software with Pangeanic

At Pangeanic, we have always been and continue to be pioneers in the MT industry. We were the first company to commercially use statistical machine translation software (Moses). We keep ahead of the trends and are dedicated to the continuous development of translation systems.

We currently provide a powerful [neural machine translation](/language-the-basis-of-neural-machine-translation) engine and all the AI capacity to achieve **personalized MT** that guarantees data protection and privacy under different regulations. We integrate the best of technology with the best of human ingenuity (human-in-the-loop).

Pangeanic's various systems, platforms, and translation tools include:

### COR: translation management

[Pangeanic's COR technology](https://pangeanic.com/translation-technology/cor-integrate-translation-technology-in-your-processes/) is a project management environment that integrates a set of synchronized web tools specifically designed to optimize translation processes and streamline workflows.

The system is stored in the cloud and the user can access it from anywhere in the world, and at any time. It reinforces sales, marketing, and localization. 

### PangeaMT

Pangeanic's [MT software](https://languagetechnology.pangeanic.com/)can process and deliver a translation in seconds. At the same time, it allows content to be anonymized according to the requirements of the GDPR and other international regulations.

Our algorithms can be trained with varying degrees of aggressiveness, have the ability to categorize documents or texts in any language, and provide sentiment analysis for discovering and understanding how your brand or company is perceived. 

As your partner, Pangeanic will support you in the expansion of your company. [Contact us](https://pangeanic.com/contact-us) and find out what our machine translation software can do for your business.

enriched_points.expanded_content: AI translation will increasingly integrate with speech recognition and natural language processing, streamlining workflows and enhancing user experience across various industries.
Advancements in neural machine translation aim to improve contextual comprehension, allowing translations to capture nuances and subtleties of languages more accurately.
The future of translation relies on synergistic collaboration between human linguists and AI, ensuring quality and cultural sensitivity in machine-generated translations.
Efforts will focus on refining algorithms to better handle idiomatic expressions, making translations more natural and culturally appropriate for diverse audiences.
Research will explore enhancing AI's capabilities in translation accuracy, contextual awareness, and addressing linguistic diversity to meet growing global communication needs.
